% Derived from dsa110-FLITS results/sightline_dm_scattering_budget.md (generated by
% galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py). DM in pc cm^-3; tau_int is the predicted intervening
% pulse-broadening at 1 GHz. Update by regenerating that table, not by hand.
\begin{deluxetable*}{lrrrrrllrr}
\tabletypesize{\scriptsize}
\tablecaption{Per-sightline dispersion and scattering budget for the twelve
co-detections, from the decomposition of Eq.~\ref{eq:dmbudget}. $\mathrm{DM_{MW}}$
is the NE2001 disk plus a $40\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$ halo; $\langle\mathrm{DM_{cos}}\rangle$
is the Macquart mean at the host redshift; $\mathrm{DM_{int}}$ is the intervening
circumgalactic column (capped at $b=0.1\,R_{\mathrm{vir}}$); $\mathrm{DM_{host}}$ is
the residual; $\tau_{\mathrm{int}}$ is the predicted intervening pulse-broadening
at $1\,\mathrm{GHz}$. ``regime'' and ``mass'' give the intervening column's
geometry (CGM, galaxy-interior, or none) and whether its halo mass is measured or
assumed. No PASS-grade measured $\tau$ is available, so the scattering comparison
is predicted-only (Figure~\ref{fig:budget}). DM in $\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$.
\label{tab:budget}}
\tablehead{\colhead{Burst} & \colhead{$z$} & \colhead{$\mathrm{DM_{obs}}$} &
\colhead{$\mathrm{DM_{MW}}$} & \colhead{$\langle\mathrm{DM_{cos}}\rangle$} &
\colhead{$\mathrm{DM_{int}}$} & \colhead{regime} & \colhead{mass} &
\colhead{$\mathrm{DM_{host}}$} & \colhead{$\tau_{\mathrm{int}}$ [ms]}}
\startdata
FRB 20220207C       & $0.043$ & 262 & 116 & 36  & 143          & CGM      & measured & $-33$  & $0.006$ \\
FRB 20220310F    & $0.479$ & 462 & 86  & 427 & 200\tablenotemark{c} & interior & assumed  & $-251$ & $0.27$ \\
FRB 20220506D       & $0.300$ & 397 & 125 & 262 & 0            & none     & \nodata  & $10$   & $0$ \\
FRB 20221113A       & $0.251$ & 411 & 132 & 217 & 23           & CGM      & assumed  & $40$   & $0.0015$ \\
FRB 20221203A    & $0.510$ & 602 & 123 & 456 & 103          & CGM      & assumed  & $-80$  & $0.008$ \\
FRB 20230307A    & $0.271$ & 610 & 78  & 235 & 368\tablenotemark{c} & interior & measured & $-70$  & $1.4$ \\
FRB 20230325A      & \nodata\tablenotemark{p} & 912 & 108 & \nodata & 5 & CGM & assumed & \nodata & $3.5\times10^{-5}$ \\
FRB 20230913A   & $0.302$ & 518 & 115 & 264 & 58           & CGM      & assumed  & $82$   & $0.022$ \\
FRB 20240122A       & \nodata\tablenotemark{p} & 960 & 171 & \nodata & 0 & none & \nodata & \nodata & $0$ \\
FRB 20240203A & $0.074$ & 272 & 116 & 62  & 0            & none     & \nodata  & $94$   & $0$ \\
FRB 20240229A      & $0.287$ & 491 & 78  & 250 & 0            & none     & \nodata  & $163$  & $0$ \\
FRB 20230814B  & \nodata\tablenotemark{p} & 696 & 145 & \nodata & 213\tablenotemark{c} & interior & assumed & \nodata & $0.23$ \\
\enddata
\tablenotetext{p}{Host redshift unknown (placeholder); the cosmological and host
terms cannot be computed, so this sightline is excluded from any distance-dependent
quantity.}
\tablenotetext{c}{Sightline in the galaxy-interior regime ($b<0.1\,R_{\mathrm{vir}}$);
$\mathrm{DM_{int}}$ is capped at the $b=0.1\,R_{\mathrm{vir}}$ value. The
uncapped core-extrapolated columns are $364$ (FRB 20220310F), $712$ (FRB 20230307A), and $390$
(FRB 20230814B)\,$\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$.}
\tablecomments{A negative $\mathrm{DM_{host}}$ (FRB 20220207C, FRB 20220310F, FRB 20221203A, FRB 20230307A)
means the modeled Galactic, cosmological, and intervening terms over-predict
$\mathrm{DM_{obs}}$; this occurs for the galaxy-interior sightlines where the
capped circumgalactic column is most uncertain, and the prior-predictive
sensitivity analysis quantifies $P(\mathrm{DM_{host}}<0)$ per sightline.}
\end{deluxetable*}
